def test_external_token_rejects_empty():
    r = ExternalTokenProvider().activate_from_user_input("", repo=None)
    assert r.ok is False
    assert "Kein Token" in r.message
passedtests.test_payment_provider::test_play_billing_not_available_on_desktop0.005s
📄 tests/test_payment_provider.py:10🧩 test_payment_provider
def test_play_billing_not_available_on_desktop():
    # Auf dem Desktop (kein BillingClient) meldet der Provider sauber
    # 'nicht verfuegbar', statt zu crashen.
    p = PlayBillingProvider()
    assert p.is_available() is False
    r = p.activate_from_user_input("")
    assert r.ok is False
    assert "Android" in r.message
passedtests.test_performance.TestBulkInsertPerformance::test_insert_500_contracts_under_15sR4R82.556s
📄 tests/test_performance.py:46🧩 test_performance🎯 R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_insert_500_contracts_under_15s(self) -> None:
        repo = ContractRepository(self.db)
        start = time.perf_counter()
        for i in range(500):
            repo.add(Contract(
                name=f"V{i}", category="streaming", provider="P",
                start_date=date(2025, 1, 1),
                minimum_term_months=12, notice_period_months=3,
                auto_renew_months=12, monthly_cost=9.99))
        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
        # Grosszuegiges Budget - dieser Test erkennt nur Regressionen
        # in Groessenordnungen, kein Mikro-Benchmark.
        self.assertLess(elapsed, 15.0,
                          f"500 Inserts dauerten {elapsed:.2f}s "
                          "(Budget: 15s) - Regressionsverdacht")

        start = time.perf_counter()
        for i in range(500):
            repo.add(Expense(description=f"E{i}", amount=1.0))
        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start

                          f"500 Expense-Inserts dauerten {elapsed:.2f}s")
passedtests.test_performance.TestBulkInsertPerformance::test_list_200_contracts_under_2sR4R81.146s
📄 tests/test_performance.py:71🧩 test_performance🎯 R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_list_200_contracts_under_2s(self) -> None:
        repo = ContractRepository(self.db)
        for i in range(200):
            repo.add(Contract(
                name=f"V{i}", category="strom", provider="P",
                start_date=date(2025, 1, 1),
                minimum_term_months=12, notice_period_months=3,
                auto_renew_months=12, monthly_cost=9.99))
        start = time.perf_counter()
        rows = repo.list_all()
        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
        self.assertEqual(len(rows), 200)
        self.assertLess(elapsed, 2.0,
                          f"List 200 dauerte {elapsed*1000:.0f}ms "
                          "- Regressionsverdacht")
passedtests.test_performance.TestDeadlineCalculationPerformance::test_deadline_calculation_scalesR4R80.127s
📄 tests/test_performance.py:91🧩 test_performance🎯 R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_deadline_calculation_scales(self) -> None:
        contract = Contract(
            name="X", category="strom", provider="",
            start_date=date(2010, 1, 1),
            minimum_term_months=12, notice_period_months=3,
            auto_renew_months=12, monthly_cost=10.0)
        # Vertrag laeuft seit ueber 15 Jahren -> viele Renew-Zyklen
        start = time.perf_counter()
        for _ in range(10000):
            next_cancellation_date(contract, today=date(2025, 6, 15))
        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
        self.assertLess(elapsed, 2.0,
                          f"10000 Deadline-Berechnungen: {elapsed:.2f}s")
passedtests.test_performance.TestNotesListingPerformance::test_list_attached_in_large_datasetR4R85.163s
📄 tests/test_performance.py:118🧩 test_performance🎯 R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_list_attached_in_large_dataset(self) -> None:
        repo = NoteRepository(self.db)
        # 1000 Notizen, davon 20 fuer Vertrag 42
        for i in range(1000):
            entity_type = "contracts" if i % 50 == 0 else None
            entity_id = 42 if i % 50 == 0 else None
            repo.add(Note(title=f"N{i}", content="x",
                            entity_type=entity_type, entity_id=entity_id))
        start = time.perf_counter()
        attached = repo.list_attached("contracts", 42)
        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
        self.assertEqual(len(attached), 20)
        self.assertLess(elapsed, 2.0,
                          f"Gefiltertes Listing: {elapsed*1000:.0f}ms")
passedtests.test_persistence_robustness.TestCalendarRecurrenceGuard::test_negative_recurrence_does_not_hangR1R5R80.001s
📄 tests/test_persistence_robustness.py:27🧩 test_persistence_robustness🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_negative_recurrence_does_not_hang(self) -> None:
        ev = CalendarEvent(title="kaputt", due_date=date(2020, 1, 1),
                           recurrence_days=-5)
        # Frueher: Endlosschleife. Jetzt: wie nicht-wiederkehrend behandelt.
        got = CalendarRepository._next_occurrence(ev, date(2026, 6, 1))
        self.assertIsNone(got)
passedtests.test_persistence_robustness.TestCalendarRecurrenceGuard::test_positive_recurrence_still_advancesR1R5R80.001s
📄 tests/test_persistence_robustness.py:41🧩 test_persistence_robustness🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_positive_recurrence_still_advances(self) -> None:
        ev = CalendarEvent(title="x", due_date=date(2026, 1, 1),
                           recurrence_days=30)
        got = CalendarRepository._next_occurrence(ev, date(2026, 3, 15))
        self.assertIsNotNone(got)
        self.assertGreaterEqual(got, date(2026, 3, 15))

    def test_zero_recurrence_is_safe(self) -> None:
        ev = CalendarEvent(title="x", due_date=date(2026, 6, 1),
                           recurrence_days=0)
        self.assertEqual(
            CalendarRepository._next_occurrence(ev, date(2026, 6, 1)),
            date(2026, 6, 1))
passedtests.test_persistence_robustness.TestOrphanOwnerId::test_contract_with_unknown_owner_stores_nullR1R5R80.163s
📄 tests/test_persistence_robustness.py:80🧩 test_persistence_robustness🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_contract_with_unknown_owner_stores_null(self) -> None:
        repo = ContractRepository(self.db)
        # owner_id 9999 existiert nicht -> frueher IntegrityError.
        c = repo.add(Contract(name="Strom", category="strom", owner_id=9999))
        stored = repo.get(c.id)
        self.assertIsNotNone(stored)
        self.assertIsNone(stored.owner_id)

    def test_contract_with_valid_owner_is_kept(self) -> None:

        repo = ContractRepository(self.db)
        c = repo.add(Contract(name="Handy", category="mobilfunk",
                              owner_id=m.id))
        self.assertEqual(repo.get(c.id).owner_id, m.id)

passedtests.test_persistence_robustness.TestProposalCreatedAt::test_created_at_is_loaded_as_datetimeR1R5R80.158s
📄 tests/test_persistence_robustness.py:66🧩 test_persistence_robustness🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_created_at_is_loaded_as_datetime(self) -> None:
        repo = ProposalRepository(self.db)
        repo.add(Proposal(source="mail", summary="Test",
                          target_capability="finance.add_expense"))
        loaded = repo.list()
        self.assertEqual(len(loaded), 1)
        self.assertIsInstance(loaded[0].created_at, datetime,
                              "created_at wurde nicht aus der DB geladen")
        # .date() darf nicht crashen (frueher: AttributeError auf str / None).
        self.assertIsInstance(loaded[0].created_at.date(), date)
passedtests.test_persistence_robustness.TestSoftDeletedOwnerName::test_soft_deleted_owner_name_is_hiddenR1R5R80.191s
📄 tests/test_persistence_robustness.py:113🧩 test_persistence_robustness🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_soft_deleted_owner_name_is_hidden(self) -> None:
        fam = FamilyRepository(self.db)
        m = fam.add_member(FamilyMember(name="Bernd"))
        repo = ContractRepository(self.db)
        c = repo.add(Contract(name="Strom", category="strom", owner_id=m.id))
        # Aktives Mitglied -> Name sichtbar.
        self.assertEqual(repo.get(c.id).owner_name, "Bernd")
        # Soft-Delete: owner_id bleibt erhalten, Name verschwindet (das
        # Mitglied ist auch aus der aktiven Liste raus -> konsistent).
        fam.soft_delete_member(m.id)
        got = repo.get(c.id)
        self.assertEqual(got.owner_id, m.id)
        self.assertFalse(got.owner_name,
                         "soft-geloeschtes Mitglied darf nicht mehr als "
                         "Owner-Name erscheinen")
        # Wiederherstellen bringt den Namen zurueck.
        fam.restore_member(m.id)
        self.assertEqual(repo.get(c.id).owner_name, "Bernd")

passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckDataDeletion::test_fails_when_service_missingR90.004s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:272🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_fails_when_service_missing(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            tmp = Path(t)
            (tmp / "database.py").write_text(
                "def wipe_all_data(): ...\n", encoding="utf-8")
            rep = pc.Report()
            orig = pc.REPO_ROOT
            pc.REPO_ROOT = tmp
            try:
                pc.check_data_deletion(rep)
            finally:
                pc.REPO_ROOT = orig
            self.assertTrue(any("data_deletion.py" in f.message
                                for f in rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL)))
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckDataDeletion::test_fails_when_wipe_method_missingR90.004s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:287🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_fails_when_wipe_method_missing(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            tmp = Path(t)
            (tmp / "services").mkdir()
            (tmp / "services" / "data_deletion.py").write_text(
                "x = 1\n", encoding="utf-8")
            (tmp / "database.py").write_text(
                "class Database: ...\n", encoding="utf-8")
            rep = pc.Report()
            orig = pc.REPO_ROOT
            pc.REPO_ROOT = tmp
            try:
                pc.check_data_deletion(rep)
            finally:
                pc.REPO_ROOT = orig
            self.assertTrue(any("wipe_all_data" in f.message
                                for f in rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL)))
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckDataDeletion::test_passes_when_mechanism_presentR90.004s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:253🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_passes_when_mechanism_present(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            tmp = Path(t)
            (tmp / "services").mkdir()
            (tmp / "services" / "data_deletion.py").write_text(
                "x = 1\n", encoding="utf-8")
            (tmp / "database.py").write_text(
                "class Database:\n    def wipe_all_data(self): ...\n",
                encoding="utf-8")
            rep = pc.Report()
            orig = pc.REPO_ROOT
            pc.REPO_ROOT = tmp
            try:
                pc.check_data_deletion(rep)
            finally:
                pc.REPO_ROOT = orig
            self.assertTrue(rep.by_level(pc.Level.PASS))
            self.assertEqual(rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL), [])

passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckPermissions::test_denied_permission_failsR90.001s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:80🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_denied_permission_fails(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.Report()
        pc.check_permissions(rep,
            {"android.permissions": "INTERNET, MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE"})
        fails = [f.message for f in rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL)]
        self.assertTrue(any("MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" in m for m in fails))

    def test_unknown_permission_warns(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.Report()
        pc.check_permissions(rep,
            {"android.permissions": "BLUETOOTH"})
        warns = [f.message for f in rep.by_level(pc.Level.WARN)]
        self.assertTrue(any("BLUETOOTH" in m for m in warns))
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckPermissions::test_whitelisted_passesR90.001s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:74🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_whitelisted_passes(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.Report()
        pc.check_permissions(rep, {"android.permissions": "INTERNET"})
        self.assertEqual(rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL), [])
        self.assertTrue(rep.by_level(pc.Level.PASS))

    def test_fails_below_min_target(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.Report()
        pc.check_sdk_levels(rep, {"android.api": "33", "android.minapi": "24"})
        fails = rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL)
        self.assertTrue(any("MIN_TARGET_SDK" in f.message for f in fails))

passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckSecrets::test_clean_repo_passesR90.004s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:180🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_clean_repo_passes(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            tmp = Path(t)
            p = self._temp_file(tmp, "ok.py", 'value = "harmless"\n')
            rep = pc.Report()
            orig = pc.REPO_ROOT
            pc.REPO_ROOT = tmp
            try:
                pc.check_secrets(rep, [p])
            finally:
                pc.REPO_ROOT = orig
            self.assertTrue(rep.by_level(pc.Level.PASS))
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckSecrets::test_detects_google_api_keyR90.003s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:165🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_detects_google_api_key(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            tmp = Path(t)
            p = self._temp_file(tmp, "leak.py", 'KEY = "AIza' + "x" * 35 + '"\n')
            rep = pc.Report()
            # ueberschreibe REPO_ROOT, damit relative_to klappt
            orig = pc.REPO_ROOT
            pc.REPO_ROOT = tmp
            try:
                pc.check_secrets(rep, [p])
            finally:
                pc.REPO_ROOT = orig
            fails = rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL)
            self.assertTrue(any("Secret" in f.message for f in fails))

passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckVersioning::test_numeric_version_consistent_passesR90.001s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:117🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_numeric_version_consistent_passes(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.Report()
        pc.check_versioning(
            rep,
            {"version": "1.0.0", "android.numeric_version": "2"},
            {"identity": {"version_name": "1.0.0", "version_code": 2}})
        self.assertEqual(rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL), [])
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckVersioning::test_numeric_version_mismatch_failsR90.001s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:136🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_numeric_version_mismatch_fails(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.Report()
        pc.check_versioning(
            rep,
            {"version": "1.0.0", "android.numeric_version": "3"},
            {"identity": {"version_name": "1.0.0", "version_code": 2}})
        self.assertTrue(rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL))
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCheckVersioning::test_numeric_version_missing_failsR90.001s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:125🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_numeric_version_missing_fails(self) -> None:
        # Ohne android.numeric_version leitet buildozer den versionCode aus
        # 'version' ab und weicht vom Store-Listing ab -> muss FAIL sein.
        rep = pc.Report()
        pc.check_versioning(
            rep,
            {"version": "1.0.0"},
            {"identity": {"version_name": "1.0.0", "version_code": 2}})
        fails = [f.message for f in rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL)]
        self.assertTrue(any("numeric_version" in m for m in fails))

passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCodeSmells::test_print_only_in_mobile_pathR90.004s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:211🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_print_only_in_mobile_path(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            tmp = Path(t)
            (tmp / "mobile").mkdir()
            (tmp / "tools").mkdir()
            mobile_file = tmp / "mobile" / "screen.py"
            mobile_file.write_text("print('hi')\n", encoding="utf-8")
            cli_file = tmp / "tools" / "cli.py"
            cli_file.write_text("print('hi')\n", encoding="utf-8")
            rep = pc.Report()
            orig = pc.REPO_ROOT
            pc.REPO_ROOT = tmp
            try:
                pc.check_smells(rep, [mobile_file, cli_file])
            finally:
                pc.REPO_ROOT = orig
            fails = rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL)
            # Es muss mindestens einen FAIL fuer den mobile-Pfad geben
            # und KEINEN FAIL fuer den tools-Pfad.
            self.assertTrue(any("mobile" in (f.file or "") for f in fails))
            self.assertFalse(any("tools" in (f.file or "") for f in fails))
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestCodeSmells::test_requests_verify_false_is_smellR90.004s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:233🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_requests_verify_false_is_smell(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            tmp = Path(t)
            (tmp / "services").mkdir()
            bad = tmp / "services" / "client.py"
            bad.write_text("import requests\nrequests.get('https://x', verify=False)\n",
                           encoding="utf-8")
            rep = pc.Report()
            orig = pc.REPO_ROOT
            pc.REPO_ROOT = tmp
            try:
                pc.check_smells(rep, [bad])
            finally:
                pc.REPO_ROOT = orig
            self.assertTrue(any("REQUESTS_VERIFY_FALSE" in f.message
                                for f in rep.by_level(pc.Level.FAIL)))
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestParseBuildozerSpec::test_extracts_app_blockR90.002s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:20🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_extracts_app_block(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            spec = Path(tmp) / "buildozer.spec"
            spec.write_text(
                "[app]\n"
                "version = 1.2.3\n"
                "android.api = 35\n"
                "android.permissions = INTERNET, ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE\n"
                "\n[buildozer]\n"
                "log_level = 2\n",
                encoding="utf-8")
            got = pc.parse_buildozer_spec(spec)
        self.assertEqual(got["version"], "1.2.3")
        self.assertEqual(got["android.api"], "35")
        self.assertNotIn("log_level", got)  # nicht im [app]-Block
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestParseBuildozerSpec::test_handles_missing_fileR90.001s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:36🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_handles_missing_file(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(pc.parse_buildozer_spec(Path("/no/such/file")), {})
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestParseBuildozerSpec::test_ignores_comments_and_blanksR90.002s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:39🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_ignores_comments_and_blanks(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            spec = Path(tmp) / "buildozer.spec"
            spec.write_text(
                "[app]\n"
                "# kommentar\n"
                "\n"
                "version = 0.1.0\n",
                encoding="utf-8")
            got = pc.parse_buildozer_spec(spec)
        self.assertEqual(got, {"version": "0.1.0"})
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestReportFormats::test_json_format_validR90.001s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:326🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_json_format_valid(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.Report()
        rep.add(check="x", level=pc.Level.PASS, message="a")
        out = pc.format_json(rep)
        data = json.loads(out)
        self.assertEqual(data["summary"]["pass"], 1)
        self.assertEqual(len(data["findings"]), 1)
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestReportFormats::test_summary_countsR90.001s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:318🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_summary_counts(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.Report()
        rep.add(check="x", level=pc.Level.PASS, message="a")
        rep.add(check="x", level=pc.Level.WARN, message="b")
        rep.add(check="x", level=pc.Level.FAIL, message="c")
        self.assertEqual(rep.summary(),
                         {"pass": 1, "warn": 1, "fail": 1, "total": 3})

    def test_runs_without_exception(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.run_checks(only=None)
        self.assertGreater(rep.summary()["total"], 0)
passedtests.test_playstore_check.TestRunChecksIntegration::test_subset_onlyR90.020s
📄 tests/test_playstore_check.py:342🧩 test_playstore_check🎯 R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_subset_only(self) -> None:
        rep = pc.run_checks(only=["versioning", "permissions"])
        checks_seen = {f.check for f in rep.findings}
        self.assertTrue(checks_seen <= {"versioning", "permissions"})
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_calendar_add_and_listR1R3R4R80.189s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:119🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_calendar_add_and_list(self) -> None:
        self.app.calendar.add("Zahnarzt", due_date="2030-01-01")
        view = self.app.calendar.list(horizon_days=3650)
        self.assertGreaterEqual(view["count"], 1)
        self.assertTrue(any("Zahnarzt" in (e.get("title") or "")
                            for e in view["items"]))
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_calendar_add_via_presenter_defaults_titleR1R3R4R80.152s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:145🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_calendar_add_via_presenter_defaults_title(self) -> None:
        self.app.calendar.add("", due_date="2030-05-01")
        titles = [e.get("title", "")
                  for e in self.app.calendar.list(horizon_days=3650)["items"]]
        self.assertTrue(any("Termin" in t for t in titles))
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_contacts_relation_filter_and_optionsR1R3R4R80.157s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:88🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_contacts_relation_filter_and_options(self) -> None:
        self.app.dispatch("social.add_contact",
                          {"name": "Oma", "relation": "Familie"})
        self.app.dispatch("social.add_contact",
                          {"name": "Chef", "relation": "Kollege"})
        view = self.app.contacts.list()
        self.assertEqual(view["count"], 2)
        self.assertEqual(view["relations"], ["Familie", "Kollege"])
        fam = self.app.contacts.list(relation="Familie")
        self.assertEqual(fam["count"], 1)
        self.assertEqual(fam["items"][0]["name"], "Oma")
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_contracts_add_list_filter_detail_deleteR1R3R4R80.185s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:25🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_contracts_add_list_filter_detail_delete(self) -> None:
        c = self.app.contracts
        c.add(name="Strom", category="strom", provider="SW", monthly_cost=30)
        c.add(name="Handy", category="mobilfunk", monthly_cost=20)
        self.assertEqual(c.list()["count"], 2)
        strom = c.list(category="strom")
        self.assertEqual(strom["count"], 1)
        self.assertEqual(strom["items"][0]["name"], "Strom")
        cid = strom["items"][0]["id"]
        self.assertEqual(c.detail(cid)["name"], "Strom")
        c.delete(cid)
        self.assertEqual(c.list(category="strom")["count"], 0)
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_contracts_empty_stateR1R3R4R80.214s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:38🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_contracts_empty_state(self) -> None:
        view = self.app.contracts.list()
        self.assertTrue(view["empty"])
        self.assertIn("Vertraege", view["empty_text"])
        # i18n-Key fuer lokalisierbare Leer-Anzeige (deutscher Default bleibt).
        self.assertEqual(view["empty_text_key"], "contracts.empty")
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_dashboard_summary_and_weekR1R3R4R80.177s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:152🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_dashboard_summary_and_week(self) -> None:
        summary = self.app.dashboard.summary()
        for key in ("contracts_count", "monthly_total",
                    "upcoming_deadlines", "upcoming_events"):
            self.assertIn(key, summary)
        week = self.app.dashboard.week()
        self.assertEqual(len(week["days"]), 7)
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_finance_add_and_recent_totalR1R3R4R80.180s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:133🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_finance_add_and_recent_total(self) -> None:
        f = self.app.finance
        self.assertNotIn("error", f.add("Kaffee", "3.50", "essen"))
        self.assertNotIn("error", f.add("Bahn", 9, ""))
        recent = f.recent(days=30)
        self.assertEqual(recent["count"], 2)
        self.assertEqual(recent["total"], 12.5)
        self.assertFalse(recent["empty"])
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_finance_add_rejects_bad_amountR1R3R4R80.166s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:142🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_finance_add_rejects_bad_amount(self) -> None:
        self.assertIn("error", self.app.finance.add("X", "keine-zahl"))
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_finance_list_empty_then_filledR1R3R4R80.174s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:127🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_finance_list_empty_then_filled(self) -> None:
        self.assertTrue(self.app.finance.list()["empty"])
        self.app.dispatch("finance.add_expense",
                          {"description": "Kaffee", "amount": 3.5})
        self.assertEqual(self.app.finance.list()["count"], 1)
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_interpolated_empty_views_expose_key_and_paramsR1R3R4R80.201s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:53🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_interpolated_empty_views_expose_key_and_params(self) -> None:
        cal = self.app.calendar.list(horizon_days=14)
        self.assertEqual(cal["empty_text_key"], "calendar.empty")
        self.assertEqual(cal["empty_text_params"], {"days": 14})
        fin = self.app.finance.recent(days=7)
        self.assertEqual(fin["empty_text_key"], "finance.recent_empty")
        self.assertEqual(fin["empty_text_params"], {"days": 7})
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_list_views_expose_i18n_keysR1R3R4R80.153s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:45🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_list_views_expose_i18n_keys(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(self.app.orders.list()["empty_text_key"],
                         "orders.empty")
        self.assertEqual(self.app.contacts.list()["empty_text_key"],
                         "contacts.empty")
        self.assertEqual(self.app.finance.list()["empty_text_key"],
                         "finance.empty")
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_orders_add_priority_category_and_completeR1R3R4R80.186s
📄 tests/test_presenters.py:69🧩 test_presenters🎯 R1 Aufgaben- & Tagesplanung (Familie, Rotation, Catch-Up, Kalender), R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung), R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen), R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ)
    def test_orders_add_priority_category_and_complete(self) -> None:
        o = self.app.orders
        self.assertNotIn("error",
                         o.add("Rasen", priority="hoch", category="garten"))
        order = o.list()["items"][0]
        self.assertEqual(order["priority"], "hoch")
        self.assertEqual(order["category"], "garten")
        o.complete(order["id"])
        self.assertEqual(o.list()["items"][0]["status"], "erledigt")

    def test_search_filter_only_without_query(self) -> None:
        self.app.contracts.add(name="Stromvertrag", category="strom",
                               monthly_cost=10)
        result = self.app.search.search("", category="strom")
        self.assertEqual(result["status"], "ok")
        self.assertGreaterEqual(result["count"], 1)

    def test_search_hits_and_empty(self) -> None:
        self.app.contracts.add(name="Stromvertrag", category="strom",
                               monthly_cost=10)
        self.assertEqual(self.app.search.search("Strom")["status"], "ok")
        self.assertEqual(self.app.search.search("zzzgibtsnicht")["status"],
                         "empty")
passedtests.test_presenters.TestPresenters::test_search_states_expose_message_keysR1R3R4R80.201s

    def test_search_states_expose_message_keys(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(self.app.search.search("a")["message_key"],
                         "search.too_short")
        self.assertEqual(
            self.app.search.search("zzzgibtsnicht")["message_key"],
            "search.no_hits")

passedtests.test_priority_category.TestOrderSchemaMigration::test_migration_adds_columns_and_keeps_rowsR30.117s
📄 tests/test_priority_category.py:129🧩 test_priority_category🎯 R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung)
    def test_migration_adds_columns_and_keeps_rows(self) -> None:
        db = Database(self.path)
        self.assertEqual(
            db.conn.execute("PRAGMA user_version").fetchone()[0], 3)
        orders = FamilyRepository(db).list_orders()
        self.assertEqual(len(orders), 1)
        self.assertEqual(orders[0].title, "Altauftrag")
        # Default greift fuer Bestandszeilen
        self.assertEqual(orders[0].priority, "normal")
        self.assertEqual(orders[0].category, "")
        db.close()

    def test_add_order_rejects_invalid_priority(self) -> None:

                                        {"title": "X", "priority": "egal"})

    def test_contacts_filter_by_relation(self) -> None:
        self.registry.dispatch("social.add_contact",
                               {"name": "Oma", "relation": "Familie"})
        self.registry.dispatch("social.add_contact",
                               {"name": "Chef", "relation": "Kollege"})
        result = self.registry.dispatch("social.contacts",
                                        {"relation": "familie"})
        self.assertEqual(result["count"], 1)
        self.assertEqual(result["contacts"][0]["name"], "Oma")
passedtests.test_priority_category.TestPriorityAndCategoryFilters::test_contracts_filter_by_categoryR30.163s
📄 tests/test_priority_category.py:33🧩 test_priority_category🎯 R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung)
    def test_contracts_filter_by_category(self) -> None:
        self.registry.dispatch("contracts.add", dict(
            name="Handy", category="mobilfunk", provider="Telekom",
            start_date="2025-01-01", minimum_term_months=1,
            notice_period_months=1, auto_renew_months=1, monthly_cost=20.0))
        self.registry.dispatch("contracts.add", dict(
            name="Haftpflicht", category="versicherung", provider="Allianz",
            start_date="2025-01-01", minimum_term_months=1,
            notice_period_months=1, auto_renew_months=1, monthly_cost=10.0))
        result = self.registry.dispatch("contracts.list",
                                        {"category": "versicherung"})
        self.assertEqual(result["count"], 1)
        self.assertEqual(result["contracts"][0]["name"], "Haftpflicht")
        # ohne Filter: beide
        self.assertEqual(
            self.registry.dispatch("contracts.list", {})["count"], 2)

passedtests.test_priority_category.TestPriorityAndCategoryFilters::test_order_priority_sort_orderR30.163s
📄 tests/test_priority_category.py:75🧩 test_priority_category🎯 R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung)
hoch vor mittel vor normal; bei Gleichstand nach Faelligkeit.
    def test_order_priority_sort_order(self) -> None:
        """hoch vor mittel vor normal; bei Gleichstand nach Faelligkeit."""
        self.registry.dispatch("family.add_order",
                               {"title": "C-normal", "priority": "normal",
                                "due_date": "2025-01-01"})
        self.registry.dispatch("family.add_order",
                               {"title": "A-hoch", "priority": "hoch",
                                "due_date": "2025-12-31"})
        self.registry.dispatch("family.add_order",
                               {"title": "B-mittel", "priority": "mittel",
                                "due_date": "2025-06-01"})
        orders = self.registry.dispatch("family.orders", {})["orders"]
        titles = [o["title"] for o in orders]
        self.assertEqual(titles, ["A-hoch", "B-mittel", "C-normal"])
passedtests.test_priority_category.TestPriorityAndCategoryFilters::test_orders_filter_by_categoryR30.170s
📄 tests/test_priority_category.py:60🧩 test_priority_category🎯 R3 Kategorien & Prioritaeten (Filter/Sortierung)
    def test_orders_filter_by_category(self) -> None:
        self.registry.dispatch("family.add_member",
                               {"name": "Max", "role": "erwachsen"})
        self.registry.dispatch("family.add_order",
                               {"title": "Steuer abgeben", "assignee": "Max",
                                "category": "finanzen"})
        self.registry.dispatch("family.add_order",
                               {"title": "Rasen maehen", "assignee": "Max",
                                "category": "garten"})
        result = self.registry.dispatch("family.orders",
                                        {"category": "garten"})
        self.assertEqual(result["count"], 1)
        self.assertEqual(result["orders"][0]["title"], "Rasen maehen")
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestBuildHtml::test_renders_markdownR7R90.001s
📄 tests/test_privacy_policy.py:44🧩 test_privacy_policy🎯 R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung), R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_renders_markdown(self) -> None:
        out = pp.build_html("## Abschnitt\n\n- Punkt eins")
        self.assertIn("<h2", out)
        self.assertIn("<li>Punkt eins</li>", out)
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestBuildHtml::test_self_contained_documentR7R90.001s
📄 tests/test_privacy_policy.py:34🧩 test_privacy_policy🎯 R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung), R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_self_contained_document(self) -> None:
        out = pp.build_html("# Titel\n\nText.", title="Datenschutz")
        self.assertIn("<!doctype html>", out)
        self.assertIn('<article class="doc">', out)
        self.assertIn("<title>Datenschutz</title>", out)
        # DOC_CSS eingebettet -> kein externer Request
        self.assertIn(".doc", out)
        self.assertNotIn("href=\"http", out.split("<article")[0]
                         .replace('content="index,follow"', ""))
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestCheck::test_finalized_policy_no_warning_about_placeholdersR7R90.015s
📄 tests/test_privacy_policy.py:69🧩 test_privacy_policy🎯 R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung), R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_finalized_policy_no_warning_about_placeholders(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            repo = self._repo_with_policy(Path(t), "Vollstaendig ausgefuellt.")
            issues = pp.check(repo)
            self.assertFalse(any("Vorlagen-Platzhalter" in m for _, m in issues))
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestCheck::test_missing_file_is_errorR7R90.001s
📄 tests/test_privacy_policy.py:57🧩 test_privacy_policy🎯 R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung), R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_missing_file_is_error(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            issues = pp.check(Path(t))
            self.assertTrue(any(sev == "error" for sev, _ in issues))
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestCheck::test_placeholders_warnR7R90.014s
📄 tests/test_privacy_policy.py:62🧩 test_privacy_policy🎯 R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung), R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_placeholders_warn(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as t:
            repo = self._repo_with_policy(Path(t), "Anbieter [ANBIETER_NAME].")
            issues = pp.check(repo)
            self.assertTrue(any("Vorlagen-Platzhalter" in m for _, m in issues))
            self.assertFalse(any(sev == "error" for sev, _ in issues))

    def test_clean_text_returns_empty(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(pp.find_placeholders("Alles ausgefuellt."), [])
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestFindPlaceholders::test_detects_template_tokensR7R90.001s
📄 tests/test_privacy_policy.py:15🧩 test_privacy_policy🎯 R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung), R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_detects_template_tokens(self) -> None:
        text = "Anbieter: [ANBIETER_NAME], Ort [PLZ_ORT]."
        self.assertEqual(pp.find_placeholders(text),
                         ["[ANBIETER_NAME]", "[PLZ_ORT]"])
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestFindPlaceholders::test_ignores_markdown_linksR7R90.001s

    def test_ignores_markdown_links(self) -> None:
        text = "Siehe [services/gate.py](../services/gate.py) und [ANBIETER]."
        self.assertEqual(pp.find_placeholders(text), ["[ANBIETER]"])
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestFindPlaceholders::test_unique_and_sortedR7R90.001s
📄 tests/test_privacy_policy.py:24🧩 test_privacy_policy🎯 R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung), R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_unique_and_sorted(self) -> None:
        text = "[B] [A] [B]"
        self.assertEqual(pp.find_placeholders(text), ["[A]", "[B]"])
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestRealRepo::test_real_build_produces_articleR7R90.002s
📄 tests/test_privacy_policy.py:83🧩 test_privacy_policy🎯 R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung), R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_real_build_produces_article(self) -> None:
        md = pp.POLICY_MD.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        out = pp.build_html(md)
        self.assertIn('<article class="doc">', out)
passedtests.test_privacy_policy.TestRealRepo::test_real_check_has_no_errorsR7R90.011s
📄 tests/test_privacy_policy.py:78🧩 test_privacy_policy🎯 R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung), R9 Play-Store-Release (Check, Store-Listing, Release-Gate, Build/AAB)
    def test_real_check_has_no_errors(self) -> None:
        # Vorlage -> Warnungen erlaubt, aber keine harten Fehler.
        issues = pp.check()
        self.assertEqual([i for i in issues if i[0] == "error"], [])
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesManager::test_create_appears_and_becomes_activeR5R70.004s
📄 tests/test_profiles.py:39🧩 test_profiles🎯 R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung)
    def test_create_appears_and_becomes_active(self) -> None:
        result = self.mgr.create("Anna")
        self.assertEqual(result["active"], "Anna")
        self.assertTrue(result["restart_required"])
        self.assertEqual(self.mgr.active(), "Anna")
        names = {p["name"] for p in self.mgr.list()}
        self.assertIn("Anna", names)
        self.assertIn("", names)            # Default bleibt wählbar
        # State-Verzeichnis wurde angelegt -> list_profiles erkennt es.
        self.assertTrue((self.base / ".alltagshelfer-state-Anna").is_dir())
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesManager::test_default_is_active_initiallyR5R70.001s
📄 tests/test_profiles.py:33🧩 test_profiles🎯 R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung)
    def test_default_is_active_initially(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(self.mgr.active(), "")
        listed = self.mgr.list()
        self.assertTrue(any(p["name"] == "" and p["active"] for p in listed))
        self.assertEqual(listed[0]["label"], DEFAULT_LABEL)
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesManager::test_env_var_overrides_pointerR5R70.003s
📄 tests/test_profiles.py:74🧩 test_profiles🎯 R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung)
    def test_env_var_overrides_pointer(self) -> None:
        self.mgr.switch("Anna")
        os.environ["ALLTAGSHELFER_PROFILE"] = "ci"
        try:
            self.assertEqual(self.mgr.active(), "ci")
        finally:
            os.environ.pop("ALLTAGSHELFER_PROFILE", None)
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesManager::test_invalid_name_rejectedR5R70.001s
📄 tests/test_profiles.py:65🧩 test_profiles🎯 R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung)
    def test_invalid_name_rejected(self) -> None:
        self.assertIn("error", self.mgr.create("   "))
        self.assertIn("error", self.mgr.create("!!!"))
        self.assertEqual(self.mgr.active(), "")     # nichts gewechselt
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesManager::test_name_is_sanitizedR5R70.004s
📄 tests/test_profiles.py:70🧩 test_profiles🎯 R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung)
    def test_name_is_sanitized(self) -> None:
        self.mgr.create("Anna Meier!")              # -> AnnaMeier
        self.assertEqual(self.mgr.active(), "AnnaMeier")
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesManager::test_switch_changes_active_and_persistsR5R70.007s
📄 tests/test_profiles.py:50🧩 test_profiles🎯 R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung)
    def test_switch_changes_active_and_persists(self) -> None:
        self.mgr.create("Anna")
        self.mgr.create("Bob")
        self.mgr.switch("Anna")
        self.assertEqual(self.mgr.active(), "Anna")
        # Frische Instanz liest denselben Pointer -> Persistenz.
        again = ProfilesManager(base_dir=self.base,
                                pointer_path=self.base / "active.json")
        self.assertEqual(again.active(), "Anna")
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesManager::test_switch_to_defaultR5R70.005s

    def test_switch_to_default(self) -> None:
        self.mgr.create("Anna")
        self.mgr.switch("")
        self.assertEqual(self.mgr.active(), "")
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesModule::test_capabilities_use_system_prefixR5R70.002s
📄 tests/test_profiles.py:99🧩 test_profiles🎯 R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung)
    def test_capabilities_use_system_prefix(self) -> None:
        names = {c.name for c in self.mod.get_capabilities()}
        self.assertEqual(names, {"system.profiles", "system.profile_create",
                                 "system.profile_switch"})
        self.assertTrue(all(n.startswith("system.") for n in names))
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesModule::test_create_invalid_returns_errorR5R70.001s
📄 tests/test_profiles.py:115🧩 test_profiles🎯 R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung)
    def test_create_invalid_returns_error(self) -> None:
        self.assertIn("error", self.mod._cap_create("###"))
passedtests.test_profiles.TestProfilesModule::test_list_create_switch_flowR5R70.006s
📄 tests/test_profiles.py:105🧩 test_profiles🎯 R5 Datenpersistenz & Mehrgeraete-Sync (FileSync/HttpSync, Konflikt, Re-Entry, TLS/Token), R7 Datenschutz & Sicherheit (Policy, Data-Safety, Consent, SQLCipher, Loeschung)
    def test_list_create_switch_flow(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(self.mod._cap_list()["active"], "")
        created = self.mod._cap_create("Anna")
        self.assertEqual(created["active"], "Anna")
        listing = self.mod._cap_list()
        self.assertEqual(listing["active"], "Anna")
        self.assertIn("Anna", {p["name"] for p in listing["profiles"]})
        self.mod._cap_switch("")
        self.assertEqual(self.mod._cap_list()["active"], "")
passedtests.test_requirements_coverage.TestRequirementsCoverage::test_every_requirement_has_at_least_one_testR8R100.001s
📄 tests/test_requirements_coverage.py:46🧩 test_requirements_coverage🎯 R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ), R10 QA / Testuebersicht (Protokoll- & Dashboard-Generatoren)
    def test_every_requirement_has_at_least_one_test(self) -> None:
        covered = {req for reqs in FILE_REQUIREMENTS.values() for req in reqs}
        missing = sorted(set(REQUIREMENTS) - covered)
        self.assertEqual(missing, [],
                         f"Anforderungen ohne zugeordneten Test: {missing}")
passedtests.test_requirements_coverage.TestRequirementsCoverage::test_mapped_files_exist_and_contain_testsR8R100.147s
📄 tests/test_requirements_coverage.py:60🧩 test_requirements_coverage🎯 R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ), R10 QA / Testuebersicht (Protokoll- & Dashboard-Generatoren)
    def test_mapped_files_exist_and_contain_tests(self) -> None:
        problems: list[str] = []
        for stem in sorted(FILE_REQUIREMENTS):
            path = _locate(stem)
            if path is None:
                problems.append(f"{stem}: Datei fehlt in tests/ bzw. "
                                f"tests/concept/")
            elif not _has_test_function(path):
                problems.append(f"{stem}: enthaelt keinen test_*-Fall")
        self.assertEqual(problems, [], "Defekte Test-Zuordnungen:\n  "
                         + "\n  ".join(problems))
passedtests.test_requirements_coverage.TestRequirementsCoverage::test_mapping_uses_only_known_requirementsR8R100.001s
📄 tests/test_requirements_coverage.py:52🧩 test_requirements_coverage🎯 R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ), R10 QA / Testuebersicht (Protokoll- & Dashboard-Generatoren)
    def test_mapping_uses_only_known_requirements(self) -> None:
        known = set(REQUIREMENTS)
        unknown = sorted({req for reqs in FILE_REQUIREMENTS.values()
                          for req in reqs if req not in known})
        self.assertEqual(unknown, [],
                         f"Mapping referenziert unbekannte Anforderungen: "
                         f"{unknown}")
passedtests.test_requirements_coverage.TestRequirementsCoverage::test_requirement_catalog_is_completeR8R100.001s
📄 tests/test_requirements_coverage.py:72🧩 test_requirements_coverage🎯 R8 Stabilitaet & Tests (Smoke, Integration, GUI-Boot, Property/Fuzz, Negativ), R10 QA / Testuebersicht (Protokoll- & Dashboard-Generatoren)
    def test_requirement_catalog_is_complete(self) -> None:
        # R1..R10 muessen lueckenlos definiert sein.
        expected = {f"R{i}" for i in range(1, 11)}
        self.assertEqual(set(REQUIREMENTS), expected,
                         "REQUIREMENTS-Katalog ist nicht R1..R10")
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderPersistence::test_clock_change_does_not_resendR20.006s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:149🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_clock_change_does_not_resend(self) -> None:
        # Erst melden, dann simulieren wir einen Zeitsprung, indem sich
        # die verbleibenden Tage aendern (z. B. DST/Datumswechsel). Da der
        # Marker datumsfrei ist, darf dieselbe Erinnerung nicht erneut
        # feuern - auch nicht nach einem Neustart.
        sched1, _ = self._scheduler([_make_event("Garantie", 4)])
        sched1.check_now()
        sched2, notifier2 = self._scheduler([_make_event("Garantie", 3)])
        self.assertEqual(sched2.check_now(), [])
        self.assertEqual(notifier2.calls, [])
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderPersistence::test_corrupt_state_file_is_ignoredR20.005s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:167🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_corrupt_state_file_is_ignored(self) -> None:
        self.state_path.write_text("nicht-json{", encoding="utf-8")
        sched, notifier = self._scheduler([_make_event("Steuer", 1)])
        # Defekte Datei -> leere Menge -> Meldung feuert dennoch.
        self.assertEqual(sched.check_now(), ["Steuer"])
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderPersistence::test_new_event_after_restart_still_firesR20.007s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:160🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_new_event_after_restart_still_fires(self) -> None:
        sched1, _ = self._scheduler([_make_event("Alt", 2)])
        sched1.check_now()
        sched2, notifier2 = self._scheduler([
            _make_event("Alt", 2), _make_event("Neu", 6)])
        self.assertEqual(sched2.check_now(), ["Neu"])
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderPersistence::test_seen_markers_survive_restartR20.006s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:139🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_seen_markers_survive_restart(self) -> None:
        events = [_make_event("TUEV faellig", 5)]
        sched1, notifier1 = self._scheduler(events)
        self.assertEqual(sched1.check_now(), ["TUEV faellig"])
        self.assertTrue(self.state_path.exists())
        # "Neustart": frische Instanz mit demselben State-Pfad.
        sched2, notifier2 = self._scheduler(events)
        self.assertEqual(sched2.check_now(), [])
        self.assertEqual(notifier2.calls, [])
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderPersistence::test_without_state_path_refires_after_restartR20.004s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:173🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_without_state_path_refires_after_restart(self) -> None:
        # Ohne Persistenzpfad bleibt das alte In-Memory-Verhalten: ein
        # "Neustart" verliert die Marker und meldet erneut.
        events = [_make_event("Fluechtig", 3)]
        n1 = _RecordingNotifier()
        ProactiveScheduler(ModuleRegistry(), notifier=n1, warn_within_days=14,
                           extra_event_sources=[_event_source(events)]
                           ).check_now()
        n2 = _RecordingNotifier()
        triggered = ProactiveScheduler(
            ModuleRegistry(), notifier=n2, warn_within_days=14,
            extra_event_sources=[_event_source(events)]).check_now()
        self.assertEqual(triggered, ["Fluechtig"])
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderTriggering::test_future_event_phrasingR20.002s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:69🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_future_event_phrasing(self) -> None:
        sched, notifier = self._scheduler([_make_event("TUEV faellig", 5)])
        triggered = sched.check_now()
        self.assertEqual(triggered, ["TUEV faellig"])
        self.assertIn("in 5 Tagen", notifier.calls[0][1])
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderTriggering::test_multiple_due_events_all_triggerR20.003s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:85🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_multiple_due_events_all_trigger(self) -> None:
        sched, notifier = self._scheduler([
            _make_event("Termin A", 1),
            _make_event("Termin B", 2),
            _make_event("Termin C", 7),
        ])
        triggered = sched.check_now()
        self.assertEqual(set(triggered), {"Termin A", "Termin B", "Termin C"})
        self.assertEqual(len(notifier.calls), 3)
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderTriggering::test_new_event_after_first_check_still_firesR20.002s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:104🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_new_event_after_first_check_still_fires(self) -> None:
        # Dedup darf nur fuer bereits gesehene Erinnerungen greifen -
        # ein neu hinzugekommenes Ereignis muss weiterhin melden.
        events = [_make_event("Erste", 3)]
        notifier = _RecordingNotifier()
        sched = ProactiveScheduler(
            ModuleRegistry(), notifier=notifier, warn_within_days=14,
            extra_event_sources=[_event_source(events)])
        sched.check_now()
        notifier.calls.clear()
        events.append(_make_event("Zweite", 6))
        triggered = sched.check_now()
        self.assertEqual(triggered, ["Zweite"])
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderTriggering::test_overdue_event_phrasingR20.002s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:80🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_overdue_event_phrasing(self) -> None:
        sched, notifier = self._scheduler([_make_event("Steuer", -3)])
        sched.check_now()
        self.assertIn("ueberfaellig", notifier.calls[0][1])
passedtests.test_scheduler_reminders.TestReminderTriggering::test_reminder_not_repeated_on_recheckR20.002s
📄 tests/test_scheduler_reminders.py:95🧩 test_scheduler_reminders🎯 R2 Erinnerungen & Benachrichtigungen (Scheduler, Persistenz, Systemzeit)
    def test_reminder_not_repeated_on_recheck(self) -> None:
        # Eine einmal gemeldete Erinnerung darf bei einem erneuten Check
        # (z. B. naechster Scheduler-Tick) nicht erneut feuern.
        sched, notifier = self._scheduler([_make_event("Garantie", 4)])
        sched.check_now()
        notifier.calls.clear()
        sched.check_now()
        self.assertEqual(notifier.calls, [])

    def test_today_event_phrasing(self) -> None:
        sched, notifier = self._scheduler([_make_event("Geburtstag", 0)])
        sched.check_now()
        self.assertIn("heute faellig", notifier.calls[0][1])

📄 tests/test_search_filters.py:99🧩 test_search_filters🎯 R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen)
Suchwort und Filter wirken als UND-Verknuepfung.
    def test_search_combines_query_and_filter(self) -> None:
        """Suchwort und Filter wirken als UND-Verknuepfung."""
        result = self._search(query="hardware", category="mobilfunk")
        # 'hardware' trifft nur die Elektronik-Ausgabe -> Kategorie passt nicht
        self.assertEqual(result["count"], 0)
passedtests.test_search_filters.TestSearchFilters::test_search_empty_query_with_filter_lists_filteredR40.220s
📄 tests/test_search_filters.py:91🧩 test_search_filters🎯 R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen)
Filter ohne Suchwort listet die gefilterten Treffer.
    def test_search_empty_query_with_filter_lists_filtered(self) -> None:
        """Filter ohne Suchwort listet die gefilterten Treffer."""
        result = self._search(category="elektronik")
        self.assertNotIn("error", result)
        sources = {h["source"] for h in result["hits"]}
        self.assertEqual(sources, {"expenses"})
        self.assertEqual(result["count"], 1)
passedtests.test_search_filters.TestSearchFilters::test_search_filters_by_categoryR40.256s
📄 tests/test_search_filters.py:55🧩 test_search_filters🎯 R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen)
Kategorie-Filter liefert nur Treffer der gewaehlten Kategorie.
    def test_search_filters_by_category(self) -> None:
        """Kategorie-Filter liefert nur Treffer der gewaehlten Kategorie."""
        result = self._search(query="telekom", category="termin")
        sources = {h["source"] for h in result["hits"]}
        self.assertEqual(sources, {"calendar"})
        # mobilfunk trifft Vertrag UND Ausgabe, sonst nichts
        result = self._search(query="telekom", category="mobilfunk")
        sources = {h["source"] for h in result["hits"]}
        self.assertEqual(sources, {"contracts", "expenses"})
passedtests.test_search_filters.TestSearchFilters::test_search_filters_by_date_rangeR40.227s
📄 tests/test_search_filters.py:65🧩 test_search_filters🎯 R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen)
Termine/Ausgaben ausserhalb des Zeitraums fehlen.
    def test_search_filters_by_date_range(self) -> None:
        """Termine/Ausgaben ausserhalb des Zeitraums fehlen."""
        result = self._search(query="telekom",
                              date_from="2025-06-01", date_to="2025-12-31")
        # Im Fenster: Termin (06-15) und Hardware-Ausgabe (08-10).
        ids = {(h["source"], h["title"]) for h in result["hits"]}
        self.assertIn(("calendar", "Buergeramt Telekom-Sache"), ids)
        self.assertIn(("expenses", "Telekom Hardware"), ids)
        # Ausserhalb: Vertrag (01-01), Roaming (01-20), und alle Quellen
        # ohne Datum (family, proposals) werden ausgeschlossen.
        self.assertNotIn(("expenses", "Telekom Roaming"), ids)
        sources = {h["source"] for h in result["hits"]}
        self.assertNotIn("contracts", sources)
        self.assertNotIn("family", sources)
        self.assertNotIn("proposals", sources)
passedtests.test_search_filters.TestSearchFilters::test_search_filters_by_statusR40.178s
📄 tests/test_search_filters.py:81🧩 test_search_filters🎯 R4 Suche & Filter (Volltextsuche, grosse Datenmengen)
Status-Filter liefert z.B. nur offene Vorschlaege.
    def test_search_filters_by_status(self) -> None:
        """Status-Filter liefert z.B. nur offene Vorschlaege."""
        result = self._search(query="telekom", status="offen")
        sources = {h["source"] for h in result["hits"]}
        self.assertEqual(sources, {"proposals"})
        # active trifft nur den Vertrag
        result = self._search(query="telekom", status="active")
        sources = {h["source"] for h in result["hits"]}
        self.assertEqual(sources, {"contracts"})
